summary.rationale: The workflow now captures the specific layout failure modes discovered during the songs work, especially redundant labels, width-first fit, and screenshot-backed row checks.

notes: The workflow contract now carries visual verification structure instead of only prose guidance.
The updated prompts direct design and verification attention to real layout regressions seen in dense operator surfaces.
Planner and Builder guidance reduce the chance that data-shape and render changes get entangled in one opaque task.
The workflow remains lightweight while becoming more specific about practical visual quality.
The rules prefer calm, width-aware, information-dense layouts over decorative spacing fixes.

next_steps: If the verify checklist becomes common across projects, consider promoting the representative-row checks into a reusable template or action.
